question: What is Novo Nordisk's technical/options/quant setup?

findings.claim: Three distinct capitulation-level selling events occurred: (1) Jul 29-30 2025 (-21.8% then -7.2%), (2) Feb 3-5 2026 (-14.6%, -6.1%, -8.3% over 3 days), and (3) Feb 23-24 2026 (-16.4%, -2.6%). Each on 3-5x average volume.

Recent volume is 51% above average (1.02M vs 673K avg), indicating sustained selling pressure, not exhaustion. True capitulation typically ends with a massive volume spike followed by declining volume — this pattern has not yet completed.
findings.data: Average daily volume (full 251-day period): 673,281. Average daily volume (last 20 days): 1,018,800. Volume ratio: +51.3%. The Feb 23 event at 5.0x avg volume ($3.37M shares) was the single highest-volume selloff but was followed by continued selling at elevated volume, suggesting forced/institutional liquidation rather than panic retail selling.

findings.claim: The Feb 2026 crash was likely triggered by CagriSema Phase 3 (REDEFINE-4) trial disappointment: drug achieved only 23% weight loss vs Zepbound's 25.5%, missing primary endpoint. This was followed by Ozempic failing Alzheimer's trials.
findings.data: CagriSema was positioned as Novo's key weapon to recapture market share from Eli Lilly's Zepbound. Phase 3 REDEFINE-4 results: CagriSema achieved 23% weight loss over 84 weeks vs Zepbound's 25.5%, missing the study's primary endpoint. Separately, oral Ozempic failed to slow cognitive decline in Alzheimer's patients in two large-scale trials. JPMorgan downgraded from Overweight to Neutral, cutting PT from 350 to 250 DKK. Deutsche Bank cut from Buy to Hold, PT 400->275 DKK.
